text.title: Semantic Tokens: Four-Stage Pipeline and Gotchas
text.summary: Four-stage token pipeline (author, export, transform, consume), agent roles per stage, naming governance rules, and LLM gotchas that break token system consistency or accessibility.

section.title: Four-Stage Pipeline
section.p: Author in Figma Variables → export via REST API → transform with Style Dictionary → consume in CSS/Swift/Compose. Agents excel at stages 2-3 and at lint enforcement in stage 4. Keep semantic naming decisions in a human design council; agents propose, humans ratify.

rule.statement: Snapshot every mode in visual regression (Chromatic, Percy) on every token pipeline run; broken dark mode is the most common token-system regression.
rule.rationale: Dark and high-contrast mode changes are invisible in code diff — only visual comparison catches an accidental color swap. Automated visual regression at the CI level is the only reliable safety net.

rule.statement: Use a data-theme attribute switcher (e.g., [data-theme="dark"]) rather than separate stylesheets; eases mid-page mode previews and iframe embedding.
rule.rationale: Separate stylesheets require re-loading or toggling link elements, which causes a flash of unstyled content. Attribute-based switching is instantaneous and composable within a single document.

rule.statement: Treat brand modes as config data, not code: adding a new white-label tenant must require only a config row in the token source, not a deploy.
rule.rationale: Code-based brand forks create N+1 maintenance burden — every system update must be applied to every brand fork. Config-overlay brand modes keep the system unified.

example.note: Mode coverage: "Given figma-variables.json from collection Colors with modes Light, Dark, HC, list semantic tokens missing in any mode. Propose a value using WCAG-AA contrast against the corresponding surface.* token in that mode. Do not invent reference tokens; reuse existing palette."
Style Dictionary config: "Convert tokens/semantic.json to style-dictionary config that emits tokens.css with [data-theme='dark'] { ... } blocks per mode and tokens.swift with a Color.Theme struct."

section.title: AI-Agent Gotchas

antipattern.description: Agent minting new reference colors instead of reusing existing palette when adding a token.
antipattern.reason: Reference token proliferation defeats the palette abstraction. Lint must reject reference tokens that duplicate existing palette entries within a configurable delta.

antipattern.description: Agent duplicating all tokens when asked to add a brand mode instead of overlaying only the delta values.
antipattern.reason: Full duplication creates N copies of every token, each requiring independent maintenance. A mode overlay contains only the tokens that differ from the base mode.

antipattern.description: Style Dictionary configs generated by agents missing platform-specific transforms (e.g., size/pxToPt for iOS).
antipattern.reason: Without the iOS pxToPt transform, point values are treated as pixel values, producing layout bugs that are only visible on physical devices — not in simulators.

antipattern.description: Agent writing CSS like var(--color-blue-500) directly in component files.
antipattern.reason: Direct reference-token usage in components bypasses the semantic layer and breaks on theme switch. Lint rule must redirect to the semantic equivalent (e.g., var(--action-primary)).

section.reference: CI gate that fails the build when any semantic token is missing a mode value
